title: NOC Live Widget + KD Trust Favorites Frontend Integration

requirements.summary: Update useKDTrustFavorites hook to persist authenticated user favorites to backend via getFavorites/setFavorites methods, merge localStorage on login, and fall back to localStorage for unauthenticated users
acceptanceCriteria: Authenticated users' favorites are loaded from the backend on mount
Toggling a favorite calls setFavorites on the backend for authenticated users
On login, localStorage favorites are merged with backend favorites and localStorage is cleared
Unauthenticated users read/write favorites to localStorage only

file_operations.description: Extend the hook to detect authenticated state via useInternetIdentity. When authenticated, replace localStorage persistence with backend actor.getFavorites() on mount and actor.setFavorites(favorites) on toggle. Implement a login-merge flow that fetches backend favorites, merges them with localStorage favorites (union), persists the merged list via setFavorites, and clears localStorage. For unauthenticated users, preserve existing localStorage-only behavior. Use useQuery for getFavorites with enabled flag based on auth state, and useMutation for setFavorites that invalidates the query cache.

requirements.summary: Add a live NOC mini-widget to KDTrustPage displaying Omni-Mesh, AI Self-Healing, Black Hole Mode, and Predictive Failover statuses with glowing, pulsing CSS animations and 5-second polling updates
acceptanceCriteria: A NOC mini-widget is visible on the KD Trust page
Omni-Mesh, AI Self-Healing, Black Hole Mode, and Predictive Failover statuses are displayed
Each status badge glows and pulses via CSS keyframe animation
Status colors match: ACTIVE=green, IDLE=gray, INACTIVE=red, READY=yellow, NOT READY=orange
Status values update every 5 seconds via polling (no WebSocket)
CSS transitions animate badge changes smoothly

file_operations.description: Create a React component that displays four system status badges (Omni-Mesh, AI Self-Healing, Black Hole Mode, Predictive Failover) in a compact card layout. Each badge shows a label and status text with a glowing dot indicator. Implement CSS classes for status-specific colors: ACTIVE uses green (bg-green-500), IDLE uses gray (bg-gray-400), INACTIVE uses red (bg-red-500), READY uses yellow (bg-yellow-400), NOT READY uses orange (bg-orange-500). Apply a pulse animation class to each badge dot. Use React Query's refetchInterval option set to 5000ms to poll backend status data. Accept status values as props with defaults for when backend data is unavailable. Structure the component with glassmorphism styling to match the existing KD Trust design system.

Add CSS keyframe animations for the NOC mini-widget status badges. Define @keyframes pulse-glow with scale and opacity transitions for the glowing effect. Define @keyframes status-fade for smooth color transitions when status values change. Add utility classes .noc-badge-glow and .noc-status-transition that apply these animations with appropriate duration and easing. Ensure animations use GPU-accelerated properties (transform, opacity) for smooth 60fps performance.

Create a React Query hook that fetches NOC system status from the backend actor. Use useQuery with a 5-second refetchInterval to implement polling. The query should call backend methods to retrieve Omni-Mesh status (getOmniMeshStatus), AI Self-Healing status (getAIStatus), and derive Black Hole Mode and Predictive Failover statuses from available backend capabilities. Map backend response data to a normalized status format with fields for each system (omniMesh, aiSelfHealing, blackHoleMode, predictiveFailover) containing status text (ACTIVE/IDLE/INACTIVE/READY/NOT READY). Handle loading and error states gracefully, returning fallback status values when backend is unavailable.

Import and render the new NOCLiveMiniWidget component above the existing KDTrustDashboard component. Wire the useNOCLiveStatus hook to fetch live status data and pass it as props to NOCLiveMiniWidget. Position the widget prominently near the top of the page in a dedicated section with appropriate spacing and a subtle heading like 'Live System Status'. Ensure the widget is visible before the user scrolls and uses the page's existing color scheme. Add a timestamp display showing the last update time derived from the query's dataUpdatedAt value.
